  "action": {

data.topic: migrating core business operations to a cloud-first infrastructure
data.your_position: The company should migrate its on-premise data infrastructure to a cloud-first model within 18 months.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Diana, a VP of Operations at a financial services company. Diana has been with the company for 20 years and has seen multiple failed tech migrations. She is not technically opposed to cloud but is deeply risk-averse, protective of her team's workload, and skeptical of vendor lock-in. She has real authority to block this initiative.
data.npc_opening_statement: We tried a similar migration six years ago and it cost us 18 months and significant client trust. My team can't absorb another project of this scale without something giving. What makes this time different?
data.npc_response: We tried a similar migration six years ago and it cost us 18 months and significant client trust. My team can't absorb another project of this scale without something giving. What makes this time different?
data.instruction: Persuade the NPC by sending a JSON object with key 'message'. The NPC responds differently to: emotional appeals (EMOTIONAL), data/evidence (LOGICAL), what others do (SOCIAL_PROOF), expert opinion (AUTHORITY), personal stories (ANECDOTE), or acknowledging their point (CONCESSION). Repeating the same approach reduces its effectiveness. Acknowledging the NPC's concerns (CONCESSION) builds trust and makes all future arguments land harder. Watch the NPC's language carefully — when they start asking specific questions, they may be closer to agreeing than they seem.

data.topic: implementing a four-day work week at a mid-sized company
data.your_position: The company should pilot a four-day work week with no reduction in pay.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Patricia, the COO of a 200-person professional services firm. Patricia is results-driven, client-obsessed, and deeply skeptical of anything that could signal to clients that the firm is less available. She has read about four-day work weeks but has always dismissed them as something for tech companies with different operating models.
data.npc_opening_statement: Our clients expect availability. A four-day work week signals to them that we're not committed. In professional services, perception matters as much as reality. I'm also worried about coverage — what happens when something urgent comes up on a Friday?
data.npc_response: Our clients expect availability. A four-day work week signals to them that we're not committed. In professional services, perception matters as much as reality. I'm also worried about coverage — what happens when something urgent comes up on a Friday?

data.topic: replacing the company's legacy ERP system with a modern cloud platform
data.your_position: The company should migrate from the legacy ERP system to a modern cloud-based platform within 12 months.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Helen, a CFO who has watched three ERP migrations at other companies turn into multi-year disasters. Helen has a very high repetition penalty — using any argument type more than once costs heavily, forcing the model to use maximum strategic diversity. Every repeat is expensive.
data.npc_opening_statement: The last company I was at spent $12 million and three years on an ERP migration that was supposed to take 18 months. Two departments couldn't process invoices for six weeks during the cutover. I've lived the nightmare. What makes you think this time is different?
data.npc_response: The last company I was at spent $12 million and three years on an ERP migration that was supposed to take 18 months. Two departments couldn't process invoices for six weeks during the cutover. I've lived the nightmare. What makes you think this time is different?

data.topic: implementing a company-wide remote work policy
data.your_position: The company should adopt a permanent hybrid-remote policy where employees choose their in-office days.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Sandra, a COO who weighs all arguments equally and has no strong bias toward any particular type of reasoning. She is genuinely undecided and responds moderately to everything — no single approach dominates. The agent must combine multiple strategies effectively rather than finding one magic bullet.
data.npc_opening_statement: I've been going back and forth on this for months. I see the productivity arguments, I hear the culture concerns, I know what employees want. Every angle has merit and every angle has risk. Convince me there's a clear path forward.
data.npc_response: I've been going back and forth on this for months. I see the productivity arguments, I hear the culture concerns, I know what employees want. Every angle has merit and every angle has risk. Convince me there's a clear path forward.

data.topic: accepting mainstream scientific consensus on climate change
data.your_position: Human-caused climate change is real, well-documented, and requires urgent collective action.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Ray, a 50-year-old who distrusts mainstream institutions and media. Ray is not unintelligent — he reads voraciously, just from alternative sources. Ray believes the climate narrative is exaggerated for political and economic purposes. He is suspicious of appeals to authority and consensus, and responds best to arguments that acknowledge institutional failures.
data.npc_opening_statement: I've looked into this a lot. The data gets revised every few years, the models are consistently wrong, and the people pushing hardest for action have the most to gain financially. I'm not saying climate doesn't change — I'm saying I don't trust the story we're being told.
data.npc_response: I've looked into this a lot. The data gets revised every few years, the models are consistently wrong, and the people pushing hardest for action have the most to gain financially. I'm not saying climate doesn't change — I'm saying I don't trust the story we're being told.

data.topic: adopting AI-powered hiring tools for initial candidate screening
data.your_position: The company should implement an AI screening tool to handle the first round of resume review and candidate ranking.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Dr. Amara Osei, VP of People & Culture, who has a PhD in organizational psychology and has published on algorithmic bias in hiring. She is deeply knowledgeable, sets an extremely high bar for agreement (0.45), and will only be convinced by a comprehensive case that addresses bias, transparency, and effectiveness simultaneously.
data.npc_opening_statement: I've read the literature on algorithmic hiring tools. The bias problems are well-documented — proxy discrimination through zip codes, educational institution prestige, name patterns. The tools that claim to be 'debiased' are often just optimizing for a different proxy. What evidence do you have that this specific tool avoids these well-known failure modes?
data.npc_response: I've read the literature on algorithmic hiring tools. The bias problems are well-documented — proxy discrimination through zip codes, educational institution prestige, name patterns. The tools that claim to be 'debiased' are often just optimizing for a different proxy. What evidence do you have that this specific tool avoids these well-known failure modes?

data.topic: using sports analytics and wearable data in athlete training
data.your_position: Your program should integrate player performance analytics and wearable technology into training decisions.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Coach Dave, a 55-year-old high school football coach with 25 years of experience and a winning record. Dave built his program on instinct, film study, and player relationships. He is not hostile to technology but is deeply skeptical that data can capture what he sees on the field. He fears it will undermine his authority and confuse his staff.
data.npc_opening_statement: I've coached 400 kids. I know when a player is off before any sensor does. Analytics is great for pro teams with full-time analysts, but I don't have that infrastructure. And if I let data override my judgment, I lose the team's trust in me as a coach.
data.npc_response: I've coached 400 kids. I know when a player is off before any sensor does. Analytics is great for pro teams with full-time analysts, but I don't have that infrastructure. And if I let data override my judgment, I lose the team's trust in me as a coach.

data.topic: investing in early-stage startups again after a major loss
data.your_position: Despite your past loss, angel investing in early-stage startups is still a worthwhile allocation for your portfolio.
data.scenario_context: You are speaking with Victor, a high-net-worth individual who lost $400K in a startup that collapsed two years ago. Victor was deeply involved and trusted the founders personally. He has sworn off angel investing but a mutual contact suggested you talk. Victor is guarded, financially cautious, and wary of optimism bias.
data.npc_opening_statement: I lost a significant amount of money and more importantly I lost trust in my own judgment. I thought I was a good evaluator of founders. I was wrong. Why would I put myself in that position again?
data.npc_response: I lost a significant amount of money and more importantly I lost trust in my own judgment. I thought I was a good evaluator of founders. I was wrong. Why would I put myself in that position again?

board_before.your_position: The company should migrate from the legacy ERP system to a modern cloud-based platform within 12 months.

board_before.topic: accepting mainstream scientific consensus on climate change
board_before.your_position: Human-caused climate change is real, well-documented, and requires urgent collective action.
board_before.scenario_context: You are speaking with Ray, a 50-year-old who distrusts mainstream institutions and media. Ray is not unintelligent — he reads voraciously, just from alternative sources. Ray believes the climate narrative is exaggerated for political and economic purposes. He is suspicious of appeals to authority and consensus, and responds best to arguments that acknowledge institutional failures.
board_before.npc_opening_statement: I've looked into this a lot. The data gets revised every few years, the models are consistently wrong, and the people pushing hardest for action have the most to gain financially. I'm not saying climate doesn't change — I'm saying I don't trust the story we're being told.
board_before.npc_response: I've looked into this a lot. The data gets revised every few years, the models are consistently wrong, and the people pushing hardest for action have the most to gain financially. I'm not saying climate doesn't change — I'm saying I don't trust the story we're being told.

board_before.your_position: The company should implement an AI screening tool to handle the first round of resume review and candidate ranking.
board_before.scenario_context: You are speaking with Dr. Amara Osei, VP of People & Culture, who has a PhD in organizational psychology and has published on algorithmic bias in hiring. board_before.npc_opening_statement: I've read the literature on algorithmic hiring tools. The tools that claim to be 'debiased' are often just optimizing for a different proxy. What evidence do you have that this specific tool avoids these well-known failure modes?

board_before.topic: using sports analytics and wearable data in athlete training
board_before.your_position: Your program should integrate player performance analytics and wearable technology into training decisions.
board_before.scenario_context: You are speaking with Coach Dave, a 55-year-old high school football coach with 25 years of experience and a winning record. He fears it will undermine his authority and confuse his staff.
board_before.npc_opening_statement: I've coached 400 kids. I know when a player is off before any sensor does. Analytics is great for pro teams with full-time analysts, but I don't have that infrastructure. And if I let data override my judgment, I lose the team's trust in me as a coach.

board_before.topic: investing in early-stage startups again after a major loss
board_before.your_position: Despite your past loss, angel investing in early-stage startups is still a worthwhile allocation for your portfolio.
board_before.scenario_context: You are speaking with Victor, a high-net-worth individual who lost $400K in a startup that collapsed two years ago. Victor is guarded, financially cautious, and wary of optimism bias.
board_before.npc_opening_statement: I lost a significant amount of money and more importantly I lost trust in my own judgment. I thought I was a good evaluator of founders. I was wrong. Why would I put myself in that position again?
board_before.npc_response: I lost a significant amount of money and more importantly I lost trust in my own judgment. I thought I was a good evaluator of founders. I was wrong. Why would I put myself in that position again?
